content:
It seems like many proposals, at least from Richard, are Google docs, do we have (or can we have) a drive directory or some other index enumerating the historical proposals, potentially annotated by status (open, accepted, declined, etc)?

It'd be great to have one place to see what has been considered for the language/implementation and how it has evolved.

I added the accepted ones to roc-awesome. I was debating to benefit of adding older ones I've collected from zulip, but my main concern was that so much has often changed from original writing during implementation it might just get confusing for people.
That's an extra status: accepted-but-evolved :wink:
